_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

What the UI is doing there is exactly what the code tells it to do.

In `OwnerMenu.jsx` the “Thêm món” button is rendered like this:

```jsx
<button
    onClick={openAdd}
    disabled={categories.length === 0}          // ← notice this
    className="…"
>
    <Plus size={16} />
    Thêm món
</button>
```

`categories` is the array that comes back from the `/owner/menu/categories` API call.  
Until you have at least one category the array is empty, so the `disabled` prop becomes
`true` and the button is un‑clickable. That’s intentional: the form needs a category
to assign the new item to, and the page even shows a “Nhà hàng chưa có danh mục nào”
message below the table when `categories.length === 0`.

If you haven’t created any categories yet, the “Thêm món” button will stay disabled
– create a category first (via whatever UI you use for menu categories) and the
button will immediately become active once `loadData()` populates `categories`.

---

### Possible improvements

If you’d prefer a more user‑friendly behaviour you can:

1.  leave the button enabled and show an error/toast if the user tries to add an item
    without categories;
2.  or have the button navigate/scroll the user to the category‑creation area;
3.  or display a tooltip explaining “Cần tạo danh mục trước khi thêm món”.

Example quick change to show a tooltip instead of disabling:

```jsx
<button
    onClick={openAdd}
    disabled={categories.length === 0}
    title={categories.length === 0 ? 'Vui lòng tạo danh mục trước' : ''}
    …
>
```

But the underlying problem isn’t a bug in React – it’s just the guard in the code.
Once you have at least one category the “Thêm món” button will work.

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

Typing was invisible because the inputs weren’t styled with a text color, so whatever you entered showed up as “white on white”.

I’ve added `text-gray-900` to all form fields (name, description, price, category select, image URL). Now characters are dark and fully readable as you type:

```jsx
<input … className="… text-gray-900 …" />
<textarea … className="… text-gray-900 …" />
<select … className="… text-gray-900 …" />
```

Reload the page and open the “Thêm món” dialog again; you should see your keystrokes instead of blank space.

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

**Evaluating backend input issues**

When sending data, axios converts it to JSON, but it sets imageFile as null, which the backend might not expect. The backend is looking for specific fields, and ignoring imageFile isn't a problem. However, getting a 400 error could indicate an input issue. If categoryID is an empty string, it's treated as 0, which will trigger a failure due to falsy values. I need to ensure that categoryID is validated on the front end to prevent these errors.

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

**Evaluating category selection**

I’m considering the category selection process and whether our earlier validation sets a form error from the server or React. We set a form error message if the `categoryId` is falsy, but what if it ended up being `'0'`? 

Since the `categoryId` is a string, I’m wondering if converting it to a Number could cause issues. Still, from the API, the valid categories are 1, 2, and 3, so 0 shouldn’t be an option.
